s52015620.txt.findings:
FINDINGS: Lungs are clear and well inflated bilaterally with no focal
consolidation, lesions, masses, pleural effusion, or evidence of pneumothorax.
The cardiomediastinal silhouette is within normal limits.  The pleural
surfaces are unremarkable.  Minimal degenerative changes of the thoracic spine
is seen.  There is no evidence of vascular congestion, pulmonary edema.
Nipple markers were placed in order to disambiguate round opacities overlying
lateral lung fields which were seen in the first image.

s58392096.txt.findings:
FINDINGS:  The lungs are well expanded.  Lungs are clear. Cardiomediastinal
and hilar contours are unremarkable.  There is no pleural effusion or
pneumothorax. Cholecystectomy clips are noted in the right upper quadrant of
the abdomen.

s51491073.txt.findings:
FINDINGS:  As compared to the previous radiograph, pre-existing signs
indicative of pulmonary edema have moderately increased.  The heart is
markedly enlarged.  There is bilateral blunting of the costophrenic sinuses,
suggesting the presence of pleural effusions.  Overall, the radiographic
appearance of the patient has progressed.  In addition, a staple line is seen
paralleling the descending aorta.  The patient is rotated and the trachea
continues to be shifted towards the right.  The changes should receive
attention on close radiographic followup.

s53935580.txt.impression:
IMPRESSION:

As compared to the previous radiograph, the patient has received a Swan-Ganz
catheter. The course of the catheter is unremarkable, the tip of the catheter
projects in the right pulmonary artery.  Unchanged moderate cardiomegaly with
mild fluid overload but no overt pulmonary edema.  No pleural effusions.
Minimal retrocardiac atelectasis. The pacemaker is in unchanged left pectoral
position.

s57650631.txt.findings:
FINDINGS:  Repeat AP and lateral chest radiographs demonstrate a right
subclavian central venous catheter tip remains in place in the mid SVC.

There is a remnant right apical pneumothorax, unchanged. There is opacity near
the right heart border and right lower lung. Subcutaneous gas on the right
thoracic wall is unchanged.  The pulmonary vasculature is normal.  There is a
small right pleural effusion.  Rib fractures are not well seen.
s57650631.txt.impression:
IMPRESSION:
1.  Small residual right apical pneumothorax.
2.  Sequelae of known lacerations in the right lung, unchanged.

s50039783.txt.findings:
FINDINGS:  As compared to the previous image, patient has received a
nasogastric tube.  As intended, the tip of the tube is located in the middle
third of the esophagus.  The other monitoring and support devices are
constant.  Constant appearance of the cardiac silhouette and of the lung
parenchyma.  The known bilateral pleural effusions are unchanged in extent and
severity.

s58961543.txt.findings:
FINDINGS:  In comparison with study of ___, there is little change in the
severe chronic disease with pleural scarring or effusion, right basilar

s52601670.txt.findings:
FINDINGS:

PA and lateral views of the chest provided.   Volumes somewhat low with mild

convincing evidence for pneumonia or edema.  No large effusion or pneumothorax
is seen.  The cardiomediastinal silhouette is normal. Imaged osseous
structures are intact.  No free air below the right hemidiaphragm is seen.

s55275081.txt.findings:
FINDINGS:  In comparison with the study of ___, the new tracheostomy tube
appears to be in good position without evidence of pneumothorax or
pneumomediastinum.  Some indistinctness of pulmonary vessels is consistent
with elevated pulmonary venous pressure in this patient with some enlargement
of the cardiac silhouette.  The left hemidiaphragm is poorly seen, consistent
with volume loss in the lower lobe and possible small pleural effusion.

s51625619.txt.findings:
FINDINGS:

3 sequential radiographs demonstrate an enteric tube at the lower hemithorax
and partially coiling in the mid stomach with the stylet pulled back 6.0 cm
from the armored tip.  Mild pulmonary edema is improving.  There is mild
improvement of the right mid lung, though persistent in the left upper lobe,
right upper and lower lobes, concerning for multifocal pneumonia.  No
appreciable pleural effusion or pneumothorax is seen.  Heart size is stable.
s51625619.txt.impression:
IMPRESSION:


1. Improving pulmonary edema.
2. Multifocal pneumonia, with focal minimal improvement.

s52389406.txt.findings:
FINDINGS:

2 views of the chest were obtained.  The lungs are lower in volume compared to

opacities consistent with atelectasis.  No definite effusion is seen although

pneumothorax.  Heart is top-normal in size with normal mediastinal contours.
s52389406.txt.impression:
IMPRESSION:

Bibasilar opacities are most likely atelectasis.

s50647005.txt.findings:
FINDINGS:  In comparison with study of ___, the pacer device appears
unchanged.  Again there is substantial enlargement of the cardiac silhouette
with relatively mild vascular congestion, raising the possibility of
cardiomyopathy or pericardial effusion in this patient with previous CABG
procedure and intact midline sternal wires.  Mild atelectatic changes are seen
at the bases.
